skill.name: setup-pre-commit
skill.description: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged (Prettier), type checking, and tests in the current repo. Use when user wants to add pre-commit hooks, set up Husky, configure lint-staged, or add commit-time formatting/typechecking/testing.
skill.summary: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged (Prettier), type checking, and tests in the current repo.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The SKILL.md file contains documentation for setting up pre-commit hooks using Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier. All detected 'external_commands' are legitimate CLI commands (npm, npx, husky, prettier) documented as setup instructions. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding at line 3 is a false positive — no cryptographic code exists in the file. The skill is a standard development tool setup guide with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands in Setup Guide
low_findings.description: The skill documents shell commands (npx husky, npm install, prettier) that will be run on the user's system to set up pre-commit hooks. This is expected behavior for a setup automation skill, not a security concern.

content.user_title: Set Up Pre-Commit Hooks in Any Repo
content.value_statement: Setting up pre-commit hooks manually involves multiple configuration files and package installations. This skill automates the entire process, giving you Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier working in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Detects the package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun) used in your repo
Installs Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier as dev dependencies
Creates a working .husky/pre-commit hook file with typecheck and test scripts
Generates a .lintstagedrc configuration for staged file formatting
Creates a default .prettierrc if none exists in the project
Verifies the setup and runs a smoke test commit
limitations: Only works for JavaScript and TypeScript projects with a package.json
Does not customize Prettier rules beyond the provided defaults
Assumes Husky v9+ syntax (no shebang in hook files)

use_cases.title: Add Pre-Commit Hooks to a New Project
use_cases.description: Set up code quality checks before any commit is made in a fresh repository.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Development Workflow
use_cases.description: Ensure all team members run the same formatting and tests before pushing code.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering team leads and DevOps engineers

use_cases.title: Add Safety Checks to Open Source Projects
use_cases.description: Run type checks and tests on contributions before they enter the codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up pre-commit hooks in this repo using Husky and Prettier.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup for a simple project

prompt_templates.title: Detect and Configure
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect my package manager and set up Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier for pre-commit checks including typecheck and tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want the skill to auto-detect your toolchain

prompt_templates.title: Custom Hook Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Husky pre-commit hooks that run Prettier on staged files, run the typecheck script, and execute the test suite.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Existing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if pre-commit hooks are properly configured in this repo and fix any issues with the Husky or lint-staged setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Troubleshooting or auditing an existing setup

output_examples.input: Set up pre-commit hooks in this project.
output: Installing husky, lint-staged, and prettier as dev dependencies
Initializing Husky with npx husky init
Created .husky/pre-commit with lint-staged, typecheck, and test commands
Setup complete. Run a test commit to verify everything works.

output_examples.input: My repo uses pnpm. Set up pre-commit hooks.
output: Running: pnpm add -D husky lint-staged prettier

best_practices: Run lint-staged first in your pre-commit hook for fast feedback on staged files only
Use --ignore-unknown with Prettier to skip binary files gracefully
Keep the pre-commit hook fast by placing slow tests in a pre-push hook instead
anti_patterns: Do not add a shebang to the pre-commit file when using Husky v9 or later
Do not run full test suites on every commit if tests take more than a few seconds
Do not skip the prepare script in package.json — Husky requires it to initialize

faq.question: Which package managers are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports npm, pnpm, yarn, and bun. It detects which lock file is present in your project.

faq.question: Does this work with TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes. If your package.json has a typecheck script, it will be included in the pre-commit hook.

faq.question: What if my project has no tests configured?
faq.answer: The skill will omit the test command from the pre-commit hook and notify you that no test script was found.

faq.question: Will this overwrite my existing Prettier config?
faq.answer: No. The skill only creates .prettierrc if no Prettier configuration already exists in your project.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Husky v9 and later?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates hook files without shebangs, which is the Husky v9+ convention.

faq.question: Can I customize the lint-staged glob patterns?
faq.answer: The skill creates a default config that formats all files. You can edit .lintstagedrc afterward to add file-specific patterns.
